message:
fix(rust): replace unwrap calls with safe alternatives in search, export, and timeline

In extract_search_terms(), two .unwrap() calls on chars.next() inside
the quoted-phrase and word-token parsing loops are replaced with
`if let Some(n)` guards. Although the iterator is peeked before
advancing, the unwrap-free pattern is more defensive and avoids a
potential panic if the iterator is somehow exhausted between peek
and next.

In run_export_html(), three serde_json::to_string_pretty().unwrap()
calls — for plan mode, dry-run mode, and JSON output mode — are
replaced with .unwrap_or_else(|_| "{}".to_string()). This ensures
that a serialization failure (however unlikely for serde_json::Value)
produces a valid empty JSON object on stdout rather than crashing.

In run_timeline(), the .unwrap() on and_hms_opt(0, 0, 0) is replaced
with .expect("valid start of day") to provide a descriptive panic
message. Midnight (0,0,0) is always a valid time, so this cannot fail,
but expect documents the invariant clearly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
